vaccines.name: Tetanus and Diphtheria (Td)
vaccines.recommendation: Booster doses are recommended every 10 years.

vaccines.name: Pertussis (Whooping Cough)
vaccines.recommendation: A single adult dose (Tdap) is recommended if not previously received in adulthood.

vaccines.name: Measles, Mumps, and Rubella (MMR)
vaccines.recommendation: Recommended, for adults born in or after 1970 who are susceptible to these viruses (no documented proof of immunization or prior illness).

vaccines.name: Varicella (Chickenpox)
vaccines.recommendation: Two, doses are recommended for all susceptible adults.

vaccines.name: Influenza (Flu)
vaccines.recommendation: Recommended, annually for all adults.

vaccines.name: COVID-19
vaccines.recommendation: Annual, updated vaccines are recommended to protect against circulating variants.

vaccines.name: Human Papillomavirus (HPV)
vaccines.recommendation: Recommended, and publicly funded up to age 26, and available up to age 45 for individuals with ongoing risk of exposure.

vaccines.name: Shingles (Herpes Zoster)
vaccines.recommendation: A two-dose series of the Shingrix vaccine is recommended for adults 50 years of age or older.

vaccines.name: RSV (Respiratory Syncytial Virus)
vaccines.recommendation: Recommended, for older adults, typically starting between ages 60 and 75, depending on provincial coverage.

vaccines.name: Tetanus and Diphtheria (Td)
vaccines.recommendation: Booster doses are recommended every 10 years.

vaccines.name: COVID-19
vaccines.recommendation: At least 1 dose of the current updated COVID-19 vaccine is recommended annually for all adults.

vaccines.name: Influenza (Flu)
vaccines.recommendation: 1 dose is recommended every flu season for all adults.

vaccines.name: Pertussis (Whooping Cough)
vaccines.recommendation: A single adult dose (Tdap) is recommended if not previously received in adulthood.

vaccines.name: Shingles (Shingrix)
vaccines.recommendation: 2 doses separated by 2-6 months, recommended for all adults aged 50 and older.

vaccines.name: Pneumococcal (Pneumonia)
vaccines.recommendation: Recommended, for adults 65 and older, or younger adults with certain underlying health conditions or risk factors. This series can include PCV15, PCV20, PCV21, or PPSV23.

vaccines.name: RSV (Respiratory Syncytial Virus)
vaccines.recommendation: 1 dose recommended for adults aged 75 and older, or for adults aged 50 through 74 who have specific underlying medical conditions.

vaccines.name: Hepatitis B
vaccines.recommendation: Recommended for all adults aged 19 through 59, and for adults 60 and older with known risk factors.

vaccines.name: HPV (Human Papillomavirus)
vaccines.recommendation: Recommended for everyone through age 26. It may also be recommended for adults aged 27 to 45 based on shared clinical decision-making with a healthcare provider.

vaccines.name: MMR (Measles, Mumps, Rubella) & Chickenpox (Varicella)
vaccines.recommendation: Required for adults without evidence of immunity or prior vaccination.

vaccines.name: Hepatitis A
vaccines.recommendation: Recommended for adults who request it or those with certain risk factors/medical conditions.

vaccines.name: SR (Sarampión y Rubéola)
vaccines.recommendation: The Measles and Rubella vaccine is provided to adults who do not have a documented, completed childhood series.

vaccines.name: Td (Tétanos y Difteria)
vaccines.recommendation: The Tetanus and Diphtheria vaccine is given as a primary series if missing, or as a booster shot every 10 years.

vaccines.name: Influenza Estacional
vaccines.recommendation: An annual seasonal flu shot is recommended for high-risk adults, including pregnant women, healthcare workers, and individuals with underlying chronic conditions (like diabetes or asthma).

vaccines.name: COVID-19
vaccines.recommendation: Routine or annual boosters are integrated into the public health guidelines, prioritized similarly to influenza.

vaccines.name: VPH (Virus del Papiloma Humano)
vaccines.recommendation: While, primarily targeted at young teens, catch-up human papillomavirus vaccination strategies apply to specific at-risk adult populations.

vaccines.name: Neumocócica Polisacárida / Conjugada
vaccines.recommendation: The Pneumococcal vaccine is given to prevent severe pneumonia. A single primary dose is given at age 65, with a revaccination booster 5 years later for individuals with high-risk health factors.

vaccines.name: Influenza Estacional
vaccines.recommendation: Unlike the younger bracket, the seasonal flu vaccine is recommended annually for all citizens over the age of 60, regardless of health status.

vaccines.name: Td (Tétanos y Difteria)
vaccines.recommendation: Maintenance boosters continue every 10 years.

vaccines.name: Hepatitis B
vaccines.recommendation: Routinely administered to adults working in the healthcare sector, dialyzed patients, or other high-exposure groups.

vaccines.name: Rabies
vaccines.recommendation: Provided proactively to agricultural workers or veterinarians in specific rural municipalities.
